notes: Cross-jurisdiction alias table for open-finance retrieval ablation experiment (docs/specs/workstream-brain/experiment-retrieval-ablation.md, Arm D). Each entry maps a canonical regulatory axis to alternative terms different jurisdictions use for the same concept. The retriever expands every A-side axis with its aliases before running BM25 / cosine on B-side axes, so 'same axis, different words' pairs (Malaysia mandated FSP vs UK ASPSP, KYC vs CDD, etc.) get surfaced even when neither embedding-similarity nor keyword-overlap alone would fire.

entries.notes: Different regulators use different terms for the customer's authorisation to share their data.
PSD2 term (PISP) vs BIS/global term (write access) vs functional description.
PSD2's AISP concept — the read-only side of open banking, distinct from payment initiation.
TSP (HKMA), TPP (PSD2), 'data consumer' (BNM Open Finance) all refer to the entity receiving customer data via API.
PSD2's ASPSP == BNM's mandated FSP == the bank exposing customer data via API.
AML/CFT vocabulary — CDD (FATF/Basel) and KYC (US) refer to the same customer identity verification process.
SCA (PSD2) is functionally MFA. Different jurisdictions bind this differently to fraud liability.
BNM MCIPD says 'customer information'; PDPA / GDPR say 'personal data'; global usage varies.
Reporting obligations after a security or operational incident — every regulator has one, terminology varies.
PSD2 RTS, HKMA Open API technical requirements, MAS API playbook — all refer to the API interface specification layer.
BCBS/BoE 'operational resilience' overlaps heavily with older 'business continuity' vocabulary.
The suite of granular consent-management controls BNM Open Finance introduces — HKMA/MAS treat this more coarsely.
Regulatory framing of institution-vendor relationships. BCBS Principle 7 uses 'third-party dependency'; BNM Outsourcing PD uses 'outsourcing arrangement'.
Overlapping vocabulary — 'cybersecurity' (US) vs 'cyber risk' (Basel) vs 'information security' (ISO 27001).
Consumer protection: routes for customer redress after harm. Different regulators point at different institutional mechanisms.
Who at the institution is on the hook — 'board' vs 'senior management' vs 'accountable officer' all appear.
Standard cross-regulator scaffolding for how risk is governed at an institution.
Institution → regulator reporting obligations, distinct from public disclosure.
PDPA/GDPR-flavour data protection principle — collect only what's needed for the stated purpose.

entries.canonical: encryption at rest and in transit
entries.notes: Baseline technical control — different regulators specify at different granularities (BNM RMiT specifies HSM; HKMA references OAuth 2.0).
